body.title: pe_chain: write PLAYBOOK skill node after M/L/XL ticket close
body.result: closed — pe_chain coding mechanism decommissioned; ticket no longer relevant to reasoning-first direction
body.description:
After pe_chain closes a ticket, no synthesis of what was non-obvious happens — learning is discarded. Hermes's self-improvement is exactly this: post-task, analyze what happened and store a reusable skill file. PLAYBOOK memory type exists (T-igor-playbook-memory-type shipped); pe_chain should use it.

In run_pe_chain() DONE path: if basket['ticket']['size'] in ('M','L','XL'), call _call_tier2() with a prompt asking 'what non-obvious procedure or decision from this ticket would a future engineer need?' Store result as PLAYBOOK palace node at path theigors/skills/<first_tag_slug>/<ticket_id_slug>. Step is non-blocking: failure logs but does not abort chain.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py (add _pe_skill_synthesis helper + call in run_pe_chain DONE path); wild_igor/igor/tools/memory_palace.py (memory_store call)
**Design rules:** pe_chain must remain non-blocking on this step; use _call_tier2 for synthesis; write via memory_store not raw psql
**Scope boundary:** In: skill synthesis for M/L/XL on successful close only. Out: S ticket skill-gen, skill deduplication/consolidation (librarian's job), grader scoring.
**Test plan:** Mock _call_tier2 returns fixed text; verify palace node created under theigors/skills/ after M ticket close. Verify S tickets do NOT trigger skill-gen. Verify skill-gen exception does not abort run_pe_chain.
